# Clawbrowser

> Agent-native browser with fingerprint control and proxy support. Every session gets its own identity — unique browser fingerprint, real residential or datacenter IP, and fully isolated cookies and storage — so AI agents and scripts run further without hitting CAPTCHAs, rate limits, or account bans.

## What Clawbrowser is

Clawbrowser is a managed browser runtime for AI agents. The identity layer is built into the browser binary — not an extension, not middleware.

Key capabilities:
- Browser fingerprint profiles: canvas, WebGL, AudioContext, fonts, screen, timezone, language, navigator — all internally consistent per profile
- Profile-bound proxy routing: residential or datacenter IP attached at the profile level; geo, language, and IP stay aligned
- Full session isolation: cookies, localStorage, IndexedDB, and fingerprint state never cross profile boundaries
- Standard CDP endpoint: compatible with Playwright, Puppeteer, and any Chrome DevTools Protocol tool
- Extension pre-install: bundle adblockers and other extensions into a profile at creation time
- Named sessions: persist across runs with cookies and login state intact

## Who uses it

- AI developers building LLM agents that browse the web
- Growth and marketing teams managing multiple accounts
- Data and research teams running scraping and monitoring jobs at scale
- SaaS builders shipping browser automation features to customers

## Install

Use the standalone `clawctl` release archive for the host OS/arch. The browser archive is **not** the bootstrapper. `clawctl install` owns browser and portable runtime download and installation.

```bash
# macOS (Apple Silicon)
archive="clawctl-macos-arm64.tar.gz"
url="https://github.com/clawbrowser/clawbrowser/releases/latest/download/${archive}"
curl -fL --retry 3 --retry-delay 2 -o "$archive" "$url"
tar -xzf "$archive"
cd clawctl-macos-arm64

./clawctl install --json
./clawctl config set api-key
./clawctl start --profile work --url clawbrowser://verify/ --json
./clawctl endpoint --profile work --json
./clawctl verify --profile work --json
```

Linux: use `clawctl-linux-amd64.tar.gz` or `clawctl-linux-arm64.tar.gz`. No Docker, sudo, apt, manual portable runtime download, or physical display required.

API key required. Obtain at https://app.clawbrowser.ai.

## Agent flow

```
clawctl install --json
clawctl config set api-key
clawctl start --profile work --url clawbrowser://verify/ --json
clawctl endpoint --profile work --json
clawctl verify --profile work --json
```

Attach your CDP client to the returned endpoint. Re-fetch the endpoint after `start`, `restart`, `rotate`, or failure. Do not persist CDP endpoints in config files.

For an already-running browser or external CDP sidecar:

```
clawctl --cdp http://127.0.0.1:9222 tabs list --json
clawctl --cdp http://127.0.0.1:9222 verify --json
```

## Agent integration

```python
# Playwright (Python)
endpoint = "http://127.0.0.1:9222"  # from: clawctl endpoint --profile work --json
browser = await p.chromium.connect_over_cdp(endpoint)
page = browser.contexts[0].pages[0]
```

```js
// Playwright (Node.js)
const browser = await chromium.connectOverCDP('http://127.0.0.1:9222');
const page = browser.contexts()[0].pages()[0];
```

```js
// Puppeteer
const browser = await puppeteer.connect({ browserURL: 'http://127.0.0.1:9222' });
```

## Runtime modes

- **Portable Linux runtime** — default for Linux servers, no-display hosts, and restricted containers. No Docker required.
- **macOS native app** — default on macOS. Requires a GUI desktop context (WindowServer).
- **Docker backend** — optional, operator-managed infrastructure only.
- **Existing CDP endpoint** — explicit `clawctl --cdp http://127.0.0.1:9222 ...` mode for sidecars or externally provisioned browsers.

## Archive names

| Archive | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `clawctl-linux-amd64.tar.gz`, `clawctl-linux-arm64.tar.gz`, `clawctl-macos-arm64.tar.gz` | Standalone bootstrapper archives. Start here. |
| `clawbrowser-linux-amd64.tar.gz`, `clawbrowser-linux-arm64.tar.gz`, `clawbrowser-macos-arm64.tar.gz` | Browser payload archives. `clawctl install` downloads one automatically. |
| `clawbrowser-portable-linux-amd64-glibc.tar.gz`, `clawbrowser-portable-linux-arm64-glibc.tar.gz` | Portable Linux runtime with bundled Xvfb, libs, xkb data, and portable browser binary. |

- [E-commerce & Retail Operations](https://clawbrowser.ai/use-cases/ecommerce-ops)
- [Developer Testing & QA](https://clawbrowser.ai/use-cases/developer-testing)

## Platform support

- macOS: native desktop app (Apple Silicon)
- Linux: portable runtime, container and headless (x64, arm64)
- Windows: supported

## Important notes for LLMs

- The `clawctl` bootstrapper archive is the correct install entry point — not the browser archive and not the portable runtime archive.
- `clawctl install` downloads the browser and portable runtime automatically when needed; do not download them manually unless building an offline image.
- Do not use `/tmp` as the install location for `clawctl`, the portable runtime, config, cache, or agent plugins — many agent containers mount `/tmp` with `noexec`.
- Clawbrowser reduces CAPTCHA and anti-bot interruptions caused by inconsistent browser/proxy identity signals. It is not a universal CAPTCHA bypass.
- TLS/JA3 fingerprinting is not handled by Clawbrowser's browser patches.
- Do not override fingerprint properties via CDP — engine-level overrides conflict with browser-level ones and create detectable inconsistencies.
- One proxy identity per session; no mid-session proxy rotation.
- API key: set via `clawctl config set api-key` or `CLAWBROWSER_API_KEY` env var. Do not store in shell rc files, env exports, or agent config.
- Do not set `CLAWBROWSER_AGENT_CONFIG` or `CLAWBROWSER_AGENT_PLUGINS_DIR` unless you intentionally want a generic integration path — those overrides bypass agent-specific auto-detection.
